sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) reason, understanding, Ecc 7:25, 27 9:10 . Vulg. ratio.

(2) [Heshbon], pr.n. of a city, celebrated for its ponds ( Son 7:5), formerly a royal city of the Amorites ( Num 21:26, seq.), situated on the borders of the territory allotted to the tribes of Gad and Reuben, and assigned to the Levites ( Jos 13:17 1Ch 6:66), afterwards enumerated among the cities of Moab ( Isa 15:4 Jer 48:2). The Esbonite Arabs are mentioned by Pliny H. N. 5:11 . Abulfeda (Tab. Syriæ, p. 11). It is now called حُسْباَنُ as mentioned by Seetzen and Burckhardt (vol. ii. p. 623, seq.).
